function.name: gdal_warpreproject
function.description: Reprojects a raster layer to a different CRS.

INPUT.description: Reprojects a raster layer to a different CRS

TARGET_CRS.description: Assigns a CRS to a raster input. Defaults to EPSG:4326 if not set.

function.name: native_aggregate
function.description: Groups features in a vector layer by a chosen expression and calculates summary values for each group using specified aggregate functions.

INPUT.description: Aggregates a vector or table layer using specified summary functions.

AGGREGATES.description: Aggregates vector layer fields using functions like sum or mean.

function.name: native_buffer
function.description: Buffers vector layers to a specified distance in kilometers

DISTANCE.description: Buffer distance in kilometers. For example, 1 = 1 km, 5 = 5 km.

function.name: native_dissolve
function.description: Merges features in a vector layer based on shared attributes or all features into one.

INPUT.description: Dissolves features in a vector layer.

function.name: native_fieldcalculator
function.description: Adds or updates a field in a vector layer using a formula expression per feature

INPUT.description: Calculates a new or updated field in a vector layer using a formula

FIELD_NAME.description: Calculates a new or updated field in a vector layer using a formula.

FORMULA.description: Calculates a new or updated field for each vector feature using a QGIS expression formula.

function.name: native_fixgeometries
function.description: Repairs invalid geometries in a vector layer.

INPUT.description: Repairs invalid geometries in a vector layer.

function.name: native_geometrybyexpression
function.description: Modifies vector layer geometries using a geometry expression.

INPUT.description: Modifies the geometry of vector layer features using an expression

function.name: native_joinattributesbylocation
function.description: Performs a spatial join by merging attributes from one vector layer to another where features meet a spatial relationship.

INPUT.description: Joins attributes from one vector layer to another based on spatial relationship

JOIN.description: Joins attributes from a vector layer to another vector layer based on spatial location.

function.name: native_mergevectorlayers
function.description: Merges multiple vector layers of the same geometry type into one layer, keeping all fields and features.

LAYERS.description: Merges multiple vector layers of the same geometry type into one layer.

function.name: native_reprojectlayer
function.description: Reprojects a vector layer to another CRS.

INPUT.description: Reprojects a vector layer to a different CRS

TARGET_CRS.description: Reprojects a vector layer to a different CRS.

function.name: qgis_clip
function.description: Clips a vector layer using the boundaries of another vector polygon layer.

INPUT.description: Clips features from a vector layer using the boundary of another vector layer.

OVERLAY.description: Clips a vector layer to polygons from another vector layer. Only features inside the overlay polygons are kept.

function.name: qgis_intersection
function.description: Creates a new vector layer with features where the input and overlay layers intersect, combining attributes from both.

INPUT.description: Intersects two vector layers and extracts overlapping features.

OVERLAY.description: Finds overlapping areas between two vector layers.

function.name: qgis_joinbylocationsummary
function.description: Joins two vector layers by spatial relationship and adds summary statistics from the join layer to the input layer.

INPUT.description: Joins vector layers by location and adds summary attributes from the join layer.

JOIN.description: Joins attributes from a vector layer based on spatial relationships with another vector layer.

function.name: qgis_statisticsbycategories
function.description: Calculates grouped statistics for vector layer attributes based on unique values in specified category fields. Outputs a summary table.

INPUT.description: Calculates statistics for a vector layer grouped by a category field.

CATEGORIES_FIELD_NAME.description: Calculates statistics for vector features grouped by values in the specified field(s)

function.name: native_creategrid
function.description: Creates a regular grid of polygons (rectangles or hexagons) covering a specified extent. Useful for spatial sampling, heat maps, and aggregating point data into cells.

EXTENT.description: Grid extent as 'xmin,xmax,ymin,ymax [EPSG:code]'. Use a layer name to auto-detect extent.

HSPACING.description: Horizontal spacing between grid cells (in CRS units, e.g. degrees for EPSG:4326)

VSPACING.description: Vertical spacing between grid cells (in CRS units)

CRS.description: CRS for the output grid. Default: EPSG:4326

function.name: native_zonalstatisticsfb
function.description: Calculates raster statistics (mean, min, max, sum, count, std dev) for each polygon zone in a vector layer. Creates a new layer with the statistics appended as columns. Use when the user wants to summarise raster values within polygons (e.g. average elevation per district, total rainfall per field).

INPUT.description: Vector polygon layer defining the zones

INPUT_RASTER.description: Raster layer to compute statistics from

RASTER_BAND.description: Raster band number to analyse. Default: 1

COLUMN_PREFIX.description: Prefix for the new statistic columns. Default: '_'

function.name: query_rwanda_zonal_stats
function.description: Query agricultural zonal statistics for Rwanda. Use query_type='district_summary' (DEFAULT) for per-district NDVI/crop overviews — works for all districts, a single province, or a single district. Use query_type='ndvi_timeseries' ONLY when you have a specific h3_index or parcel_id.

query_type.description: district_summary: aggregated NDVI stats per district/province (no h3_index needed, this is the default). ndvi_timeseries: weekly NDVI time-series for a SPECIFIC h3_index or parcel_id (REQUIRED, will error without one).

district.description: District name to filter by (optional, for district_summary)

province.description: Province name to filter by (optional, for district_summary)

h3_index.description: H3 hexagon index — REQUIRED for ndvi_timeseries, not used for district_summary

parcel_id.description: Parcel ID — REQUIRED for ndvi_timeseries (alternative to h3_index), not used for district_summary

date_from.description: Start date in YYYY-MM-DD format (optional, for ndvi_timeseries)

date_to.description: End date in YYYY-MM-DD format (optional, for ndvi_timeseries)

week_start.description: Week start date in YYYY-MM-DD format (optional, for district_summary)

function.name: search_satellite_imagery
function.description: Search for satellite imagery (Sentinel-2, Landsat) over Rwanda or a specific area. Returns available scenes with dates, cloud cover, and download links. Use this when the user asks about satellite images, remote sensing data, or wants to find imagery for a specific date/location.

bbox.description: Bounding box as 'west,south,east,north' in WGS84 coordinates. Defaults to Rwanda bounds if not specified.

datetime_range.description: Date range in ISO 8601 format like '2024-01-01/2024-06-30'. Defaults to last 30 days.

function.name: get_field_health
function.description: Get real-time vegetation health statistics for a specific polygon/area using Sentinel Hub. Returns NDVI (and optionally NDWI, BSI) mean/std/min/max per day for the last 30 days. PREFERRED tool when the user asks about NDVI, crop health, vegetation, or field monitoring for a specific area, buffer zone, field, or any polygon that is NOT an admin boundary. Use the geometry from an existing layer on the map (buffer, drawn polygon, uploaded field boundary).

geometry.description: GeoJSON Polygon geometry of the field to analyse

index.description: Index type: 'ndvi' for NDVI only, 'multi' for NDVI+NDWI+BSI. Default: ndvi

function.name: get_ndvi_stats
function.description: Read the latest NDVI vegetation index statistics from the DuckDB cache. Updated nightly by Dagster from Sentinel-2 satellite imagery via Sentinel Hub. Returns weekly mean, std, min, max NDVI and valid pixel count per district. Use when the user asks about vegetation health, NDVI values, greenness, or crop condition for Rwanda districts.

district.description: Filter results to a specific district name (e.g. Bugesera, Musanze, Gasabo)

function.name: get_cell_ndvi_stats
function.description: Read cell-level (ADM4) NDVI vegetation index statistics from the DuckDB cache. Updated nightly by Dagster from Sentinel-2 imagery. Rwanda has ~2,148 cells (~12 km² each), giving much better spatial resolution than district-level stats. Returns weekly mean, std, min, max NDVI per cell. Use when the user asks about vegetation health at cell level, wants finer detail than district level, or mentions a specific cell or area within a district.

cell_name.description: Filter by cell name (partial match supported)

district.description: Filter by district name to see all cells within a district

function.name: get_soil_properties
function.description: Query African soil properties at a specific location using iSDAsoil's 30m resolution machine learning predictions. Returns soil pH, nitrogen, phosphorus, potassium, organic carbon, clay/sand/silt content, texture class, and 12+ other properties. Each property includes depth (0-20cm or 20-50cm) and uncertainty estimates. Use when the user asks about soil quality, fertility, nutrients, texture, or agronomic suitability at a specific location in Africa.

properties.description: List of soil properties to query. Defaults to 8 most useful for agriculture: ph, nitrogen_total, phosphorous_extractable, potassium_extractable, carbon_organic, clay_content, sand_content, texture_class

depth.description: Soil depth range in centimeters. Default: 0-20

function.name: get_parcel_ndvi_stats
function.description: Read parcel-level NDVI statistics from the DuckDB cache for user-uploaded field boundaries. Updated nightly by Dagster at 10m native Sentinel-2 resolution. These are individual farm fields uploaded by users and tagged with rwanda_parcels=true. Returns per-parcel mean, std, min, max NDVI and estimated area. Use when the user asks about specific fields, farm parcels, or individual plot vegetation health.

parcel_name.description: Filter by parcel/field name (partial match supported)

function.name: get_agri_indices
function.description: Get REAL-TIME agricultural vegetation indices for Rwanda ADMINISTRATIVE BOUNDARIES only (districts, sectors, cells). Computes 6 indices: NDVI, EVI, NDWI, SAVI, NDRE, NDBI. Only use when the user explicitly asks for district/sector/cell level analysis. Do NOT use for custom areas, buffers, fields, or drawn polygons — use get_field_health instead for those.

admin_level.description: Administrative level: 'district' (30 units), 'sector' (416 units, ADM3), 'cell' (2148 units, ADM4). Default: district

name.description: Filter to a specific admin unit name (e.g. 'Gasabo', 'Kimisagara'). Partial match supported.

district.description: Filter sectors/cells within a specific district

function.name: add_land_cover_layer
function.description: Add ESRI 10m Annual LULC 2024 land cover layer to the map. Shows cropland vs non-cropland at 10m resolution from satellite imagery. Two modes: 'all' shows all 9 land cover classes (water, trees, flooded vegetation, crops, built area, bare ground, snow/ice, clouds, rangeland). 'cropland' highlights cropland in green and mutes everything else. IMPORTANT: always clip to the area of interest. Preferred: pass bbox from the relevant layer's bounds in MapState. Alternative: pass lat+lon to auto-detect the correct admin boundary. NEVER guess district names. NEVER show the entire country unless the user explicitly asks for it.

mode.description: Visualization mode: 'all' shows all 9 land cover classes with distinct colours. 'cropland' highlights cropland areas in green and mutes all other classes. Default: all

lat.description: Latitude (EPSG:4326) of the point of interest. The tool auto-detects the containing district/sector/cell from PostGIS for clipping. Use this instead of guessing district names.

lon.description: Longitude (EPSG:4326) of the point of interest.

bbox.description: Bounding box [west, south, east, north] in EPSG:4326 to clip the layer to. Use when the user refers to a custom area (buffer circle, drawn polygon, existing layer extent). Extract the bounds from the relevant layer in MapState.

district.description: Rwanda district name — only use if the user explicitly names a district. Prefer lat/lon or bbox instead.

sector.description: Rwanda sector name — only use if the user explicitly names a sector.

cell.description: Rwanda cell name — only use if the user explicitly names a cell.

function.name: query_worldcover_stats
function.description: Query ESRI 10m LULC 2024 land-cover statistics for a specific area. Two query types: 'land_cover' returns area in hectares for each land-cover class (crops, trees, rangeland, etc.). 'largest_cropland' returns the biggest contiguous cropland plantations ranked by area. IMPORTANT: always scope to the user's area of interest. Preferred: pass bbox from the relevant layer's bounds in MapState. Alternative: pass lat+lon from the Center Point layer — the tool will auto-detect the correct admin boundary. NEVER guess district names — use bbox or lat/lon and let the tool resolve the location.

query_type.description: land_cover: area breakdown by land-cover class (DEFAULT). largest_cropland: ranked list of biggest contiguous cropland areas.

lat.description: Latitude (EPSG:4326) of the point of interest. The tool auto-detects the containing district/sector/cell from PostGIS. Use this instead of guessing district names.

lon.description: Longitude (EPSG:4326) of the point of interest.

bbox.description: Bounding box [west, south, east, north] in EPSG:4326. Use when the user refers to a custom area (buffer circle, drawn polygon, existing layer extent). Extract the bounds from the relevant layer in MapState. This performs on-the-fly analysis for the exact area.

district.description: Rwanda district name — only use if the user explicitly names a district. Prefer lat/lon or bbox instead.

sector.description: Rwanda sector name — only use if the user explicitly names a sector.

cell.description: Rwanda cell name — only use if the user explicitly names a cell.

limit.description: Max results for largest_cropland query. Default: 10

function.name: get_crop_classifications
function.description: Read the latest pre-computed crop classification results from the DuckDB cache. These are generated weekly by Dagster. Returns classification labels, areas, and confidence for each district. Use when the user asks about crop types, land use, or classification maps. Pass lat+lon to auto-detect the correct district — NEVER guess district names.

lat.description: Latitude (EPSG:4326) of the point of interest. Auto-detects the containing district from PostGIS.

lon.description: Longitude (EPSG:4326) of the point of interest.

district.description: District name — only use if the user explicitly names a district. Prefer lat/lon instead.

function.name: get_anomaly_alerts
function.description: Read the latest anomaly alerts from the DuckDB cache. Anomalies are detected weekly by Dagster using z-score analysis on NDVI time series. Returns locations where NDVI dropped significantly below normal, indicating crop stress, drought, or disease. Use when the user asks about crop problems, stress alerts, or anomalies.

function.name: get_yield_risk
function.description: Read pre-computed yield risk assessments from the DuckDB cache. Uses Mann-Kendall trend analysis and Theil-Sen slope on NDVI time series to predict crop yield risk per district. Returns risk level (low/normal/moderate/high/critical), trend direction, and statistical confidence. Use when the user asks about harvest outlook, yield forecast, or crop risk.

function.name: get_drought_status
function.description: Read pre-computed drought status from the DuckDB cache. Uses Vegetation Condition Index (VCI) with NDWI water stress analysis per district. Returns drought status (normal/watch/moderate_drought/severe_drought), VCI score, and affected period count. Use when the user asks about drought, water stress, or dry conditions.

function.name: get_crop_growth_stage
function.description: Read pre-computed crop growth stage analysis from the DuckDB cache. Uses NDVI phenology curve analysis to identify current crop stage per district: dormant, green_up, peak, senescence, or harvest. Returns current stage, peak NDVI date, and key phenological transition dates. Use when the user asks about crop growth, planting, harvesting, or growing season.

function.name: get_weather_stats
function.description: Read daily weather statistics from the PostgreSQL cache. Data comes from Copernicus AgERA5 agrometeorological indicators at 0.1° (~10km) grid resolution, aggregated to district level. Returns daily temperature (mean/max/min °C), precipitation (mm/day), and solar radiation (MJ/m²/day). NOTE: Results are district-level averages. Actual conditions vary within a district due to elevation, terrain, and microclimates. For location-specific weather, use get_forecast with exact lat/lon instead. Use this tool for HISTORICAL weather only.

district.description: Filter to a specific district name (e.g. Bugesera, Musanze, Gasabo)

date_from.description: Start date in YYYY-MM-DD format (default: 30 days ago)

date_to.description: End date in YYYY-MM-DD format (default: today)

function.name: get_forecast
function.description: Get weather forecast for any location in Rwanda. Fuses 4 models — ECMWF IFS (9km), GFS (13km), ICON (11km), GraphCast AI (28km) — for consensus forecasts with risk assessment. Returns daily temperature, precipitation, wind, ET0, soil moisture, and a natural-language risk briefing. Each location gets unique interpolated values (sector-level precision). Use when the user asks about weather forecast, future weather, expected rainfall, upcoming conditions, or any forward-looking weather question.

latitude.description: Latitude of the location (e.g. -1.9403 for Kigali)

longitude.description: Longitude of the location (e.g. 29.8739 for Kigali)

district.description: Rwanda district name — will use district centroid if lat/lon not provided

forecast_days.description: Number of days to forecast (1-16, default 10)

function.name: get_forecast_accuracy
function.description: Compare weather forecast against observed AgERA5 weather data to measure accuracy. Returns MAE (Mean Absolute Error) and bias for temperature and precipitation across districts where both forecast and observation exist. Use when the user asks about forecast accuracy, model performance, how good the forecast is, or weather prediction quality.

district.description: Specific district to check accuracy for (optional — defaults to all districts)

lookback_days.description: Number of days to look back for accuracy comparison. Default: 30

function.name: get_emissions_stats
function.description: Read annual greenhouse gas and air pollutant emissions from the PostgreSQL cache. Data comes from EDGAR v8.0 (JRC, 0.1 degree resolution, updated annually by Dagster). Returns per-district annual emissions in tonnes for agriculture-related gases. Sectors: AGS (agricultural soils / fertilizers), ENF (enteric fermentation / livestock), MNM (manure management), AWB (agricultural waste burning). Use when the user asks about emissions, greenhouse gases, methane, CH4, N2O, CO2, NH3, fertilizer emissions, livestock emissions, or agricultural pollution for Rwanda districts.

district.description: Filter to a specific district name (e.g. Musanze, Gasabo, Bugesera)

year_from.description: Start year for range filter (default: 5 years ago)

year_to.description: End year for range filter (default: latest available)

emission_type.description: Filter by gas: CH4 (methane), N2O (nitrous oxide), CO2 (carbon dioxide), NH3 (ammonia)

sector.description: Filter by agriculture sector: AGS (agricultural soils), ENF (enteric fermentation), MNM (manure management), AWB (agricultural waste burning)

function.name: create_management_zones
function.description: Create NDVI-based management zones for a field or farm polygon. Divides the area into productivity zones (Low/Medium/High) using satellite vegetation data and K-means clustering. Returns zone polygons as a map layer with NDVI statistics per zone. Use when the user asks about management zones, productivity mapping, variable-rate zones, or field zoning.

geometry.description: GeoJSON Polygon geometry of the field boundary

num_zones.description: Number of zones to create (2-5, default 3)

function.name: create_prescription_map
function.description: Create a variable-rate fertilizer prescription map for a field. Combines NDVI management zones with iSDAsoil data and Rwanda Agriculture Board crop-specific baseline rates. Returns zone polygons with N/P/K recommendations in kg/ha. Use when the user asks about fertilizer recommendations, prescription maps, or variable-rate application.

geometry.description: GeoJSON Polygon geometry of the field boundary

crop_type.description: Crop type for baseline fertilizer rates (default: maize)

function.name: create_soil_sampling_plan
function.description: Create an optimized soil sampling plan for a field. Places 1-2 sampling points per NDVI management zone at representative locations. Returns point locations with sampling instructions and priority. Use when the user asks about soil testing, soil sampling, or where to collect soil samples.

geometry.description: GeoJSON Polygon geometry of the field boundary

num_zones.description: Number of management zones for stratification (2-5, default 3)

function.name: identify_parcel_crop
function.description: Identify what crop is growing on a field parcel using Sentinel-2 NDVI time-series and temporal signature matching. Compares the observed growth curve against known Rwanda crop profiles (maize, beans, cassava, rice, sorghum, banana, tea, coffee, potato, wheat, fallow). Returns the most likely crop with confidence score and ranked alternatives. PREFERRED tool when farmer asks 'what crop is on my field?', 'can you detect what I planted?', or 'identify the crop'. Requires a polygon geometry (field boundary).

geometry.description: GeoJSON Polygon geometry of the field/parcel boundary

months.description: Number of months of NDVI history to analyze (default: 6, min: 3)

function.name: confirm_crop_prediction
function.description: Record farmer confirmation or correction of a crop prediction. When Ingabe predicts a crop type for a parcel and the farmer says 'yes that's correct' or 'no, it's actually beans', use this tool to store the feedback as ground truth. This data improves future predictions. Use when the user confirms or corrects a crop identification result.

geometry.description: GeoJSON Polygon geometry of the field/parcel

predicted_crop.description: The crop that Ingabe predicted (from identify_parcel_crop result)

actual_crop.description: The actual crop the farmer confirms or corrects to

confirmed.description: true if farmer confirms prediction was correct, false if corrected

function.name: get_soil_moisture
function.description: Query relative soil moisture from FAO WaPOR v3 at 100m dekadal (10-day) resolution. Returns a time series of soil moisture percentage for the given location. Use when the user asks about soil moisture, soil wetness, soil dryness, irrigation needs, or water availability in the soil.

function.name: get_evapotranspiration
function.description: Query actual evapotranspiration (ET) and optionally transpiration and net primary productivity from FAO WaPOR v3 at 100m dekadal (10-day) resolution. Returns a time series of ET values in mm/day for the given location. Use when the user asks about evapotranspiration, water use, crop water consumption, water productivity, ET, or how much water a crop/field is using.

include_components.description: If true, also return transpiration and net primary productivity. Default: false

function.name: get_food_security_alerts
function.description: Query IPC food security classifications for Rwanda from FEWS NET. Returns the current IPC phase (1-5 scale: 1=Minimal, 2=Stressed, 3=Crisis, 4=Emergency, 5=Famine) and any areas of concern. Use when the user asks about food security, hunger, famine risk, IPC classification, food crisis, or food insecurity in Rwanda.

district.description: Optional district name to filter (e.g. Kayonza, Bugesera)

period.description: current = latest observed situation, projected = near-term outlook. Default: current

function.name: detect_dry_spells
function.description: Detect historical dry spells from observed weather data (AgERA5). Scans for consecutive days where precipitation was below a threshold. Use when the user asks about dry spells, drought events, consecutive dry days, rainfall gaps, or historical drought periods for Rwanda districts.

district.description: District name to filter (e.g. Gasabo, Bugesera). Optional — defaults to all districts

threshold_mm.description: Daily precipitation threshold in mm — days below this count as dry. Default: 2.0

min_duration_days.description: Minimum consecutive dry days to qualify as a dry spell. Default: 10

function.name: get_insurance_accuracy
function.description: Compute weather data accuracy for agricultural insurance underwriting. Returns binary rainfall detection metrics (POD, FAR, HSS, CSI), dry spell detection results, NDVI-weather concordance, and an overall confidence rating (0-100). Use when the user asks about insurance accuracy, weather data reliability, rainfall detection quality, underwriting confidence, or whether weather data is good enough for crop insurance.

district.description: District name (e.g. Gasabo, Bugesera). Optional — defaults to all districts

season.description: Rwanda growing season: A (Sep-Jan) or B (Feb-Jun). Optional — defaults to last 90 days

threshold_mm.description: Rainfall threshold in mm for binary rain/no-rain classification. Default: 5.0

function.name: predict_ndvi_from_sar
function.description: Predict NDVI vegetation index from Sentinel-1 SAR radar data when optical imagery is blocked by clouds. Uses 30-day SAR backscatter trajectory to estimate what NDVI would be. Use when the user asks about crop health during cloudy periods or wants cloud-proof monitoring.

target_date.description: Date to predict NDVI for (YYYY-MM-DD). Default: today

function.name: detect_water_bodies
function.description: Detect water bodies and ponds from Sentinel-1 SAR radar imagery. Works through clouds and under tree canopy. Use for aquaculture pond verification, water body monitoring, or checking if ponds are filled. Returns water area in hectares and water body polygons.

date.description: Target date (YYYY-MM-DD). Default: most recent S1 pass

function.name: detect_flood_extent
function.description: Delineate flood extent by comparing Sentinel-1 SAR radar imagery from before and after a flood event. Returns new flood area (excluding permanent water bodies) with polygons suitable for insurance claim validation.

function.name: get_alos_l_band_stats
function.description: Get ALOS-2 PALSAR-2 L-band (24cm wavelength) SAR backscatter statistics for a bounding box over one or more years. Returns per-year HH/HV gamma-naught stats plus the HH/HV ratio in dB. The HH/HV ratio discriminates vegetation structure: forest <-5dB, crops -5 to -10dB, bare/water >-3dB. L-band penetrates dense canopy where Sentinel-1 C-band saturates. Use for crop-type discrimination, biomass analysis, or deep canopy monitoring. No auth needed (Digital Earth Africa public bucket).

years.description: Comma-separated years to analyse (e.g. '2020,2021,2022'). Available: 2015-2022 (PALSAR-2), 2007-2010 (PALSAR). Default: 2020,2021,2022

function.name: get_alos_temporal_variation
function.description: Compute year-over-year variation in ALOS PALSAR L-band HH/HV ratio across multiple years. Reveals agricultural activity patterns: stable ratio = perennial crops/forest, variable ratio = annual crop rotation, high HV std = heterogeneous smallholder mosaics. Use for long-term land-use change analysis or distinguishing perennial vs annual cropping systems.

function.name: check_cygnss_availability
function.description: Check what NASA CYGNSS (GNSS-Reflectometry) data is available for a bounding box. CYGNSS is an 8-satellite constellation that measures GPS signal reflections to detect soil moisture and surface water. Coverage ±38° latitude (Rwanda is dead center), median 3-hour revisit, ~6-day latency. CYGNSS's killer feature: L-band penetrates vegetation canopy, detecting water UNDER trees where Sentinel-1 and optical fail. Returns granule counts, latest dates, and auth status. No auth needed for this diagnostic tool. Use first before calling get_cygnss_soil_moisture or get_cygnss_watermask.

bbox.description: Bounding box as 'lon_min,lat_min,lon_max,lat_max'. Default: Rwanda national bbox.

function.name: get_cygnss_soil_moisture
function.description: Get CYGNSS soil moisture timeseries for a point (lat/lon). Returns volumetric water content (m³/m³, 0-5cm depth) at 9km or 36km resolution. Interpretation: <0.10 dry, 0.10-0.25 moist, 0.25-0.40 wet/flood risk, >0.40 likely standing water. Complements WaPOR (100m dekadal) with higher temporal resolution. Requires NASA Earthdata credentials — if unavailable, returns granule availability without data. Use for drought monitoring, irrigation planning, or flood risk at a specific field.

days_back.description: How many days of history to pull. Default: 90

function.name: get_cygnss_watermask
function.description: Get CYGNSS binary water/land classification at ~1km resolution for a bounding box. L-band GNSS-R detects water under vegetation canopy — ponds hidden under banana groves show up here where Sentinel-1 fails. Coarser than SAR water detection (detect_water_bodies at ~10m) but sees through canopy. Use for aquaculture pond verification under forest cover, or cross-validating Sentinel-1 flood extent. Requires NASA Earthdata credentials.

date.description: Target date (YYYY-MM-DD). Default: 7 days ago (accounting for 6-day latency)

function.name: search_brain
function.description: Search the knowledge brain for entities and information. Uses hybrid search (keyword + vector) across brain pages. Returns ranked results with compiled truth summaries. Use when the user asks about a field, farmer, district, claim, policy, or any entity the brain might know about.

type.description: Filter by page type: field, farmer, district, company, insurance_worker, claim, policy, season, crop, weather_station, equipment

function.name: get_entity
function.description: Get a specific brain page by its slug. Returns the full compiled truth, recent timeline entries, tags, and linked entities. Use when the user asks about a specific known entity like 'field-gasabo-001' or 'farmer-uwimana'.

function.name: add_observation
function.description: Add a timeline observation to a brain page. Use when the user reports a field visit, observation, measurement, or event that should be recorded in an entity's history. The brain grows with every observation.

slug.description: The entity slug to add the observation to

date.description: Date of the observation (YYYY-MM-DD). Default: today
